title: Discovery, Delivery, Distribution (3Ds)
definition: The product management lifecycle: discover what to build, deliver it, distribute it to users.
whenToUse: Framing any PM work; deciding where to spend your time.
interviewPrompts: Walk me through the product development lifecycle.
Where should a PM spend most of their time?

title: Product Sense
definition: Intuition for what users need, grounded in problems they care about — not features for their own sake.
whenToUse: Evaluating ideas, prioritizing, interview case questions.
frameworkSteps: Judge if solution fits the job
interviewPrompts: How would you improve Google Maps?

title: User Centricity
definition: Building around what users value — they reciprocate attention or money only when you solve an important problem.

title: Ambiguity to Clarity
definition: The PM converts fuzzy goals into clear plans: vision → strategy → roadmap → PRD → executable work.
interviewPrompts: What does a PM actually do day to day?

title: Product Backlog Structure
definition: Epics → features → user stories, each with goals and success metrics (e.g. The Go Product car rental example).
interviewPrompts: How do you structure a product backlog?
